title: Morning Sickness: A Guide to Causes and Herbal Preparations
causes_desc: When you're pregnant, your body goes through a lot of changes. Morning sickness, which can happen at any time of day, is caused by a combination of hormonal changes and nutrient deficiencies. One of the main causes is a hormone called Human Chorionic Gonadotropin, or HCG for short. This hormone helps the fertilized egg implant in the uterus and grows the embryo, but it also makes you feel queasy and tired.  Another hormone that's to blame is Progesterone, which helps your uterus relax and get ready for the baby. Progesterone levels surge during pregnancy, and this can cause your stomach to feel upset and your body to feel tired. Estrogen, another hormone that's important for pregnancy, can also contribute to morning sickness. As estrogen levels increase, it can make your stomach contract and feel queasy.  Some people are also at risk for morning sickness if they're deficient in certain vitamins. Vitamin B6 is an important nutrient that helps your body convert food into energy, and if you don't have enough of it, you might feel queasy and tired. Vitamin B12 is also important for your body's energy levels, and a deficiency can make you feel weak and tired. If you're not eating enough foods that are rich in these vitamins, or if your body is having trouble absorbing them, you might experience more severe morning sickness.
herbs_desc: Herbs can help with morning sickness because they have natural properties that ease nausea and vomiting.  One of the best herbs for morning sickness is Zingiber officinale, or ginger. Ginger has compounds that slow down digestion and reduce inflammation in the stomach, which can help calm nausea. It's also easy to digest, making it a safe choice for pregnant women.  Mentha x piperita, or peppermint, is another herb that can help with morning sickness. Peppermint oil has a calming effect on the stomach and can help ease nausea. It can be consumed as tea, added to food, or inhaled through steam.  Foeniculum vulgare, or fennel, is a natural anti-inflammatory that can help soothe the stomach. Its seeds contain compounds that ease nausea and reduce vomiting. Fennel can be consumed as tea or added to food.  Cinnamomum verum, or Ceylon cinnamon, has a compound called cinnamaldehyde that helps ease nausea and reduce inflammation in the stomach. It can be consumed in small amounts as a tea or added to food.  Ginkgo biloba is not typically used for morning sickness. While it has been used to improve circulation and reduce inflammation, it's not a common herb recommended for morning sickness.
preparations_desc: If you're experiencing morning sickness, herbal preparations can be a big help. Ginger Tincture is one of the most effective remedies. It works by soothing your stomach and reducing nausea. The active compounds in ginger, called gingerols and shogaols, have natural anti-inflammatory properties that calm your digestive system.  Another great option is Peppermint Tea, which can help ease your stomach and reduce vomiting. The menthol in peppermint oil helps relax your stomach muscles and improves digestion.  Fennel Decoction is also beneficial, as it helps to relax the stomach and reduce nausea. Fennel seeds have a natural compound called anethole, which helps to ease digestive discomfort and relieve bloating.  Taking Vitamin B Complex Capsules can also be helpful, especially if you're experiencing fatigue or weakness along with morning sickness. Vitamin B is essential for your body's energy production, and deficiencies in B vitamins can exacerbate morning sickness symptoms.  Lastly, Lemon Balm Oil can be used in aromatherapy to help calm your mind and body. The scent of lemon balm has a soothing effect on your nervous system, which can help reduce anxiety and stress that can worsen morning sickness symptoms.
benefits_desc: Using herbs for morning sickness can be a great help. One of the main benefits is that they can provide quick relief from nausea and vomiting. Many women find that these herbs are easy to digest, even when their tummies are feeling queasy. This makes them a popular choice for morning sickness. Some herbs can also help to reduce the frequency and severity of vomiting episodes, which can be a huge relief for women who are struggling to keep food down. Additionally, these herbs can help to improve digestion and reduce stomach discomfort, making it easier for women to eat and keep their energy levels up. Some herbs may also have a calming effect, which can help to reduce stress and anxiety that can often come with morning sickness.
